full_name: IEC 62619 Edition 2.0 2022-05: Secondary cells and batteries containing alkaline or other non-acid electrolytes - Safety requirements for secondary lithium cells and batteries, for use in industrial applications

tests.test_name_en: External Short-Circuit Test (Cell or Cell Block)

conditions.duration: 6 hours or until case temperature declines by 80% of maximum temperature rise, whichever is sooner
conditions.sample_size: Not specified in excerpt (refer to full standard)

criteria.description: Short-circuit between positive and negative terminals shall not cause fire or explosion.

tests.original_text_snippet: Section 7.2.1 b): Fully charged cells are stored in an ambient temperature of 25 °C ± 5 °C. Each cell is then short-circuited by connecting the positive and negative terminals with a total external resistance of 30 mΩ ± 10 mΩ. The cells are to remain on test for 6 h or until the case temperature declines by 80% of the maximum temperature rise, whichever is the sooner.

conditions.soc: 50% capacity of rated capacity (discharged at 0.2 It A)
conditions.apparatus: Type 316 stainless steel bar, 15.8 ± 0.1 mm diameter, at least 60 mm length
conditions.orientation_cylindrical: Longitudinal axis parallel to floor, perpendicular to bar
conditions.orientation_prismatic: Both wide and narrow sides (rotate 90°), separate samples for each impact

criteria.description: An impact to the cell shall not cause fire or explosion.

tests.original_text_snippet: Section 7.2.2 b): The cell or cell block is placed on a flat concrete or metal floor. A type 316 stainless steel bar with a diameter of 15.8 mm ± 0.1 mm and at least 60 mm in length is placed across the centre of the cell. A 9.1 kg rigid mass is then dropped from a height of 610 mm ± 25 mm onto the bar placed on the sample. Each sample is to be subjected to only a single impact with separate samples to be used for each impact.

tests.test_name_en: Drop Test (Cell or Cell Block, and Battery System)

conditions.drop_count_edge_corner: 2 times per impact type (edge and corner drop test)
conditions.rest_time: Minimum 1 hour after test before visual inspection

criteria.description: Dropping the test unit shall not cause fire or explosion.

tests.original_text_snippet: Section 7.2.3.2 b): Each fully charged test unit is dropped three times from a height shown in Table 2 onto a flat concrete or metal floor. If the mass of the test unit is less than 7 kg, the test unit is dropped so as to obtain impacts in random orientations. If the mass is 7 kg or more but less than 20 kg, the test shall be performed with the test unit dropped in the bottom down direction.

tests.test_name_en: Thermal Abuse Test (Cell or Cell Block)

criteria.description: An elevated temperature exposure shall not cause fire or explosion.

tests.original_text_snippet: Section 7.2.4 b): Each fully charged cell, stabilized in an ambient temperature of 25 °C ± 5 °C, is placed in a gravity or circulating air-convection oven. The oven temperature is raised at a rate of 5 °C / min ± 2 °C /min to a temperature of 85 °C ± 5 °C. The cell remains at this temperature for 3 h before the test is discontinued.

tests.applicability: Battery systems with only single control/protection for charging voltage control

conditions.soc: Discharged to final voltage specified by manufacturer (at 0.2 It A)
conditions.charging_current: Maximum specified charging current of battery system
conditions.charging_voltage: Maximum voltage possible when original charging control does not work
conditions.termination: Until temperature reaches steady state (<10°C change in 30 min) or returns to ambient
conditions.parallel_cells: For parallel-connected cells: current = max charging current / number of parallel cells

criteria.description: Charging for longer periods than specified shall not cause fire or explosion.

exemptions: Battery systems with two or more independent protections/controls for charging voltage control may be waived (7.2.5)
Example of two independent protections: cell voltage monitoring with charge current control + diagnostic monitoring system that detects failure (7.2.5 NOTE)
tests.original_text_snippet: Section 7.2.5 b): Sample cells shall then be charged with a constant current equal to the maximum specified charging current of the battery system until the voltage reaches the maximum voltage value that is possible under the condition where the original charging control of the battery system does not work. The test shall be continued until the temperature of the cell surface reaches steady state conditions (less than 10 °C change in a 30-min period) or returns to ambient temperature.

tests.test_name_en: Forced Discharge Test (Cell or Cell Block)

conditions.soc: Discharged to final voltage specified by manufacturer (at 0.2 It A), then forced discharged
conditions.target_voltage_single: Minus upper limit charging voltage (for systems with 2+ protections or single cell)
conditions.target_voltage_multiple: Minus (n-1) × upper limit charging voltage (n = number of series cells, for systems with single/no protection)
conditions.reverse_charging_time: t = Im / It × min (if max discharging current < 1.0 It A)

criteria.description: A cell in a battery system shall withstand forced discharge without causing fire or explosion.

tests.original_text_snippet: Section 7.2.6 b): A discharged cell is subjected to a forced discharge at a constant current of 1.0 It A for a test period of 90 min. If the voltage in discharge reaches the target voltage within the test period, the voltage shall be kept at the target voltage by reducing the current for the remaining test period.

conditions.soc: Discharged at 0.2 It A to final voltage, then charged at upper limited charging voltage until current drops to 0.05 It A
conditions.apparatus: Press equipment with nitrile rubber or acrylic resin pressing jig
conditions.nickel_particle: Inserted at center of outer end positive and negative electrode pair

criteria.description: A forced internal short-circuit test shall not cause fire.

exemptions: Shall be conducted by cell manufacturer or third-party test house (7.3.2 a))
tests.original_text_snippet: Section 7.3.2 b) 2): The fixture is moved down at a speed of 0.1 mm/s monitoring the cell voltage. When a voltage drop caused by the internal short-circuit is detected, stop the descent immediately and keep the pressing jig in the position for 30 s, and then release the pressure. If the voltage drops more than 50 mV compared to the initial voltage, an internal short-circuit has been determined to have occurred.

conditions.trigger_method: One target cell heated by laser (or other method) until thermal runaway
conditions.trigger_off: Triggering source turned off after thermal runaway in target cell is initiated

criteria.description: No external fire from battery system, no battery system case rupture.
criteria.note: Fire or case rupture caused by target cell is acceptable (intentional trigger)

tests.original_text_snippet: Section 7.3.3 b): The battery system is fully charged and then left until the cells stabilize in an ambient temperature of 25 °C ± 5 °C. One cell in the battery system (target cell) is e.g. heated by laser until the cell enters into thermal runaway. After thermal runaway in the cell is initiated, the triggering source is turned off and battery system is observed for 8 h.

conditions.soc: Discharged to final voltage specified by manufacturer (at 0.2 It A)
conditions.excess_voltage: 10% above upper limit charging voltage for each cell

criteria.description: BMS terminates charging before exceeding upper limit charging voltage. No fire, no explosion.
criteria.safety_stop: If BMS fails: stop when cell voltage reaches 103% of upper limit or 1 min after exceeding

tests.original_text_snippet: Section 8.2.2 b): Sample battery systems shall then be charged at the maximum current of the recommended charger with set voltage exceeding the upper limit charging voltage by 10% for each cell in the battery system. The test shall be carried out until the BMS terminates the charging. Data acquisition/monitoring shall be continued for 1 h after charging is stopped.

conditions.soc: Discharged to final voltage specified by manufacturer (at 0.2 It A)

criteria.description: BMS shall detect overcharging current and control charging below maximum charging current. No fire, no explosion.

exemptions: May be waived if maximum charging current of upper system is lower than maximum charging current for battery system (8.2.3)
tests.original_text_snippet: Section 8.2.3 b): Sample battery systems shall then be charged at a current exceeding the maximum charging current by 20%. Data acquisition and/or monitoring shall be continued for 1 h after charging is stopped.

exemptions_summary.exemption: Battery systems with two or more independent protections/controls for charging voltage control may be waived (7.2.5)
May be waived if maximum charging current of upper system is lower than battery system maximum (8.2.3)
Shall be conducted by cell manufacturer or third-party test house as design evaluation (7.3.2)

metadata.extraction_method: Manual reading and analysis of complete .md file
metadata.notes: IEC62619 is an industrial battery safety standard. Key features include: (1) Drop test varies by mass, (2) Internal short-circuit test is design evaluation, (3) Propagation test evaluates thermal runaway containment, (4) BMS functional safety requirements with SIL target.
